title: cephalomanes ledermannii
intro: Cephalomanes ledermannii, also known as the Philippine liverwort, is a type of bryophyte, a non-vascular plant that belongs to the liverwort family. Traditionally, it has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as protecting the liver and kidneys, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Cephalomanes ledermannii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent require further scientific research.
